title: Execution Backend Configuration
description: Defines available LLM backends, their capabilities and costs, and per-persona model routing. Consumed by the governance pipeline to select models, track token usage, and enforce cost budgets.

backends.description: Named backend definitions. Keys are backend identifiers referenced by default_backend and model_assignment.

context_window.description: Maximum context window size in tokens.

cost_per_1k_input_tokens.description: Cost in USD per 1,000 input tokens.

cost_per_1k_output_tokens.description: Cost in USD per 1,000 output tokens.

capabilities.description: Model capabilities relevant for task routing (e.g., 'code-generation', 'reasoning', 'vision').

max_output_tokens.description: Maximum output tokens the model can generate in a single response.

default_backend.description: Key into 'backends' used when no persona-specific assignment exists.

model_assignment.description: Per-persona model routing. Keys are persona identifiers (e.g., 'code-manager', 'coder', 'security-review').

primary.description: Preferred backend key for this persona.

fallback.description: Backend key to use if the primary is unavailable or budget-exceeded.
